response: The internet originated in the 1960s with U.S. Department of Defense-funded ARPANET, first demonstrated in 1969, and grew through the 1970s; it was formalized in 1983 with TCP/IP protocols, and the World Wide Web was invented by Tim Berners-Lee in 1989, followed by the 1993 launch of the Mosaic browser that made it accessible to ordinary users. The late 1990s saw a dot-com boom with massive investment, many companies failing in the 2001 crash, though survivors like Amazon and Google became highly valuable, while the mid-2000s brought social media (Facebook, Twitter, YouTube) and the 2007 iPhone powered mobile internet, and AWS's 2006 launch marked the rise of cloud computing. Today, the internet connects over 5 billion people and underpins nearly all aspects of modern life, from commerce to communication to entertainment.
